title: Oil Prices Drop as Iran Opens a Key Waterway
summary: Oil prices went down a lot after Iran said it would keep the Strait of Hormuz open for ships during a ceasefire. The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow waterway where much of the world's oil travels. When it was closed, oil prices went up because less oil could get through. Now that Iran says it's open, prices fell from over $98 to $88 per barrel. This is good news for people who buy gas because lower oil prices can mean cheaper fuel. The U.S. president is happy about this, but some shipping groups are still checking to make sure it's safe. Markets around the world went up because of this news. The Strait had been closed since February when there were military strikes, causing oil to become very expensive. Even though Iran says it's open, some experts warn ships to be careful because there might still be dangers like mines.

keywords.explanation: the cost of oil that affects gas prices
a narrow waterway where lots of oil ships go
a unit used to measure oil
places where people buy and sell things, like stocks

questions.question: What happened to oil prices after Iran's announcement?
What is the Strait of Hormuz?
Why did oil prices go up before the announcement?
Who is happy about Iran's statement?
What are some shipping groups worried about?
How did global markets react to the news?

background_read: Oil is a natural resource used to make fuel for cars and planes. The Strait of Hormuz is an important route in the Middle East where many oil tankers pass through. When conflicts happen, countries might close such routes, making oil scarce and expensive. A ceasefire is when fighting stops for a while, allowing things like trade to resume. This article talks about how Iran's decision to open the Strait during a ceasefire affects oil prices and global markets.
Article_Structure: WHO: Iran and the U.S. president
WHAT: Oil prices dropped after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz is open
WHERE: Strait of Hormuz and global markets
WHY: Because opening the Strait allows more oil to flow, reducing prices

perspectives.description: Lower oil prices help consumers and boost markets
Shipping groups warn of safety risks despite the opening
